gazette: the Official Gazette dated April 5, 2023, and numbered 32154.
title: The Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between the Republic of Türkiye and the United Arab Emirates (UAE)
bodyHtml:
The "Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between the Republic of Türkiye and the United Arab Emirates (UAE)" signed in Abu Dhabi on March 3, 2023, and the "Notes" regarding amendments to the Agreement were ratified. The law regarding ratification was published in the Official Gazette dated April 5, 2023, and numbered 32154.

With the signed agreement, in terms of market access in trade in goods, 80 percent of all tariff lines are subject to concessions by the parties, while Türkiye achieves significant gains in many sectors, including the globally competitive automotive, white goods, petrochemical products, textiles and carpets, shoe production, plastic products, machinery, and various electronic devices.

With the entry into force of the Agreement, Türkiye will eliminate or reduce customs duties  on goods originating from the UAE, according to Annex 2-1A (Tariff Schedules), and the UAE will eliminate or reduce customs duties on goods originating from Türkiye, according to Annex 2-1B (Tariff Schedules). Türkiye's concessions list includes reductions specifically for additional customs duties. It is recommended to carefully review the relevant lists.

In accordance with bilateral cumulation, when inputs originating from one party are incorporated into a product in the other party, those inputs will be considered as originating from the other party. Those inputs will not be required to have undergone sufficient processing or working provided that they are processed beyond insufficient working or processing.

The TR-UAE Origin Proof Document will be used as the Origin Certificate.

The statement of origin may also be used as the origin certificate by approved exporters, regardless of the value of the relevant products.

Notification of the internal approval processes and the publication of the regulation for relevant rules of origin are expected for the Agreement to enter into force.
